what_was_done: Added BASIC keywords DO, WHILE, and ENDWHILE.
Implemented line-oriented DO..WHILE and WHILE..ENDWHILE handlers in basic_flow.s8.
Added runtime structure-scanning helpers that classify WHILE lines as either block openers or DO-terminators.
Added an integration test program sophia_basic_test_loops.bas and wired it into CTest as test_basic_loops.
Updated README.md, sophia8.md, and sophia8.context.json with the new loop feature and the related memory-map lesson.
what_was_found: Sophia8 CMP/CMPR are destructive, so multi-branch comparisons on the same register must always use a scratch copy.
Loop-structure scans must preserve caller registers across helper calls; otherwise matching logic drifts to the wrong line.
The old BASIC GOSUB/FOR stack region at 0x6A00/0x6A20 is no longer safe after code growth because assembled BASIC helper code now occupies that area.
The user POKE regression area 0x9600/0x9601 still must remain free; basic_data.s8 needed an explicit relocation gap to keep it safe.
next_to_do: Consider adding explicit memory-map reservations for BASIC runtime stack regions in the context docs.
Consider adding a regression test that exercises GOSUB/FOR together with loop blocks in one program.
Consider sorting stored BASIC lines by line number during insertion if deterministic execution order independent of entry order becomes a goal.
